title: Evangelion 222 for iPhone4 iPad AppleTV Something Else
display_title: Evangelion 222 for iPhone4 iPad AppleTV Something Else
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.22 for iPhone4/iPad/AppleTV (+Something Else)             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Mon Aug 23, 2010 1:26 am I did a bit of encoding to mess around with what my iPhone4 could actually play back. Turns out the iPhone can actually play SRT subtitles if they are multiplexed into *.MP4 correctly. This is not as clean as some of the stuff that Apple uses for the iTunes Music Store, but hey, it was free and it's Eva ! Here are the technical details of the file. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Evangelion 2.22 for iPhone4/iPad/AppleTV (+Something Els             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Aug 23, 2010 4:59 am squall.leonhart wrote: Anime is 2D, has always been 2D, and will always be 3D.

[]: Re: Evangelion 2.22 for iPhone4/iPad/AppleTV (+Something Els             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Mon Aug 23, 2010 7:01 am NAveryW wrote: There goes my me.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Mon Aug 23, 2010 9:58 pm Eva on the Go w00t!

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Tue Aug 24, 2010 7:31 am God help my poor wallet if a 3D version was ever done, god help all our wallets in fact, & my poor shelf that would rapidly fill with the various releases, J-R2, HK-R3, US-R1, UK-R2 & so on, multiply by 3D & I'd need to build a special Evangelion shelf

[]:             Quote Post by Seph » Sun Aug 29, 2010 7:37 pm I would sincerely appreciate it if you made a 1980p MP4, so that I could watch it on my PS3. Possible?

[]:             Quote Post by destiny13777 » Sun Aug 29, 2010 10:52 pm Hawq wrote: God help my poor wallet if a 3D version was ever done

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Aug 29, 2010 10:58 pm I think the part when Mari lifts her arm and the Eva copies in the background would look awesome in 3D. Or when Mari comes flying in to hit Shinji. Or when Eva-01 rips apart Eva-03...

[]:             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Mon Aug 30, 2010 1:53 am Seph wrote: I would sincerely appreciate it if you made a 1980p MP4, so that I could watch it on my PS3. Possible?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Aug 30, 2010 7:07 am What's wrong with hardsubbing it for the PS3 MP4? It's what I did when I muxed the BD-R.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Aug 30, 2010 8:23 am 720p and only 1 GB? This is awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Seph » Mon Aug 30, 2010 6:58 pm Could someone hardsub it then? I already stream it using Playstation Media Server. Thing is, it's the only movie that won't stream properly. I've streamed tons of other 1080p MKVs, but this one just refuses to cooperate.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Sep 01, 2010 4:26 am Is this really relevant to the "Rebuild of Evangelion" section? Anyway the PS3 can play AVCHD that it's a reduced version of Blu Ray used by Sony and Panasonic handcam. You can do the easy way using MultiAVCHD but it won't keep the styles of the original .ass, otherwise the hard way (which I use) is : avs2bdnxml +[url=http://forum.doom9.org/showthread.php?t=145277[/url]BDSup2Sub[/url]+ tsmuxer +[url=http://forum.doom9.org/showthread.php?t=143920[/url]AVCHDManager[/url]. Keep in mind that some edit of the .ass script is needed, especially removing the /fad tag and other weird tags is usually needed. As input you could use the Blu Ray .ts if you have an external HD with enough free space (in this way you'll basically sub the BD but you'll lose the menus that can't be converted, 2.22 is especially impossible to convert right now since it uses Java). Otherwise you can encode it by yourself but make sure to use High Profile@Level 4.1 and keep pyramid b-frames to "strict", also keep low the number of reference frames (4 for 1080p, 9 for 720p), or just grab some of the floating raws/fansub, as long as they respect the above limits they're fine (you could check them with mediainfo; if they are played by MPHC's DXVA decoder they should be fine on the PS3 as well).

[]:             Quote Post by KnifeEyeAttack » Tue Sep 14, 2010 8:59 am Best of luck, but why in god's name would you want to watch a movie this enormous on a fucking phone!?

[]:             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Tue Sep 14, 2010 9:50 am KnifeEyeAttack wrote: Best of luck, but why in god's name would you want to watch a movie this enormous on a fucking phone!?

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Tue Sep 14, 2010 12:53 pm the firsr time i saw this movie was on my ipod. my ps3 was broken so i reformat the video for the ipod classic. unlike most of the people i know i've never had any trouble watching subtitles of my ipod, so if it works on other bigger screen media more power to ya.

[]:             Quote Post by Gopherbeans » Sat Dec 25, 2010 5:34 am Please don't ruin your first day here. [url]http://wiki.evageeks.org/EvaGeeks_Forum_Rules_and_Guidelines#Other[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sat Dec 25, 2010 7:47 am I feel like I'm the only one who believes "3D" anime would suck. If Khara decided "Lets convert this to 3D so all of the 3D models pop out in your face." I'd be.. very against that decision. This is my opinion, but 3D really sucks. It doesn't make a movie better by any means. If you hated Rebuild before, you'll still hate it in 3D. You cannot make stuff on a 2D plain pop out. A lot of 1.11 and 2.22 is characters talking and CGI cars and buildings. Most of that wouldn't work that well anyway. You'd have to reanimate it entirely to get that depth. I enjoy rambling.

[]:             Quote Post by CORE » Sat Dec 25, 2010 12:31 pm The interesting thing with 3D reactions (not ticket or hardware sales) just pure response is that in parts of the world where HD TV adoption is lower 3D reaction is more positive like Eurasia/Japan specifically. While in parts of the world where HD TV adoption is higher 3D reaction is more cynical (but sales are still just as high if not higher due to stronger Home theatre markets). Its pretty interesting.. the content is going to be so regional appeal in nature for afue years because of this. Anime thou..mm its easy to forgive a drawings errors than a cameras eye but i really do agree with the general idea of a new style of technique of 2D animation would have to be developed to have it work flawless. Some thing like Increased textures and less flat surface color would go a long way to give the stereoscopic a chance to sculpt a figure out of a drawing with depth.. but i don't know how the purity heads would like that.. maybe a plastic gloss texture would be enough to keep innocently view of minds eye be happy. Basically you need line work texture to imply stereoscopic depth and anime lacks that in many important areas would have to do some radical changes to the style and would probe end up looking like glossy dolls or ruff wooden or scally dolls rather than drawings.. but that might be ok?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Dec 25, 2010 12:37 pm All I can think looking at this post (pics actually) is that it never hit me that Maya could be Shinji's twin sister; they look creepily identical in those screenshots. p.s Sharing is caring, but huuum, I got a feeling that this does not lie within the rule borders, so eh eh..
